title: Rapid Crumbly Slovakian Potato Hash
description: A quick and flavorful Slovakian dish featuring crumbly potatoes, jalapeno, and a deglaze, made FODMAP-friendly, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a satisfying snack.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the diced potatoes with 1 tablespoon of garlic-infused oil, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of garlic-infused oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced jalapeno and onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the roasted potatoes to the skillet and stir to combine with the jalapeno and onion mixture.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has almost completely evaporated and the potato mixture is crumbly, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Serve the Rapid Crumbly Slovakian Potato Hash hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
